channel.title: Tyaan Singh | Data Science Portfolio
channel.description: Recent content on Tyaan Singh | Data Science Portfolio

item.title: Shiny App - Analysis of Director Genders in Popular Films
item.author: singhtyaan@gmail.com (Tyaan Singh)

item.title: Analysis of Director Genders in Popular Films
item.author: singhtyaan@gmail.com (Tyaan Singh)
item.description: Check out the Github for this project Check out the Shiny app Overview After a bit of recent uproar around the director for the Barbie film not recieving an oscar award, I decided that it might be interesting to do a project analysing the disparity between genders of directors in popular films. When deciding which dataset to choose, I checked out a few different film sites and their rating systems. I settled on Letterboxd because it seemed to be the site with the highest number of user watches and reviews for most films compared to sites like IMDB and TMDB.

item.title: Contact
item.author: singhtyaan@gmail.com (Tyaan Singh)
item.description: The best way to get in contact with me is to email me at singhtyaan@gmail.com

item.title: CV
item.author: singhtyaan@gmail.com (Tyaan Singh)
item.description: Click to download full CV pdf Summary Self-driven and highly motivated, with a strong creative and logical mind. Passionate about mathematics and data science. Equipped with a solid foundation in analytical thinking, a degree in electronics engineering and a wide range of data analytics skills. Comfortable working both self-sufficiently and as part of a team, and adept in professional communication skills. Education Victoria University of Wellington 2015 - 2017 Bachelor of Science in Electronics and Computer Science Engineering 2018 Graduate Diploma in Music Composition Coursera Online Courses 2023 Data Analysis with R Programming by Google Microsoft Azure SQL by Microsoft

item.title: Sentiment Analysis of Financial News Headlines with Python - Part 3
item.author: singhtyaan@gmail.com (Tyaan Singh)
item.description: Check out the Github for this project This post is the first of a 3 part series. Part 1 - Scraping financial news headlines. Part 2 - Performing sentiment analysis to get sentiment scores for each headline. Part 3 - Aggregating our headline sentiment scores to create a sentiment value for each day the market is open, and checking for correlation between this and the stock price movement over the following day. This project is the first addition to my data science portfolio. The purpose of this project is to showcase web scraping, sentiment analysis, and some basic statistical analysis.

item.title: Sentiment Analysis of Financial News Headlines with Python - Part 2
item.author: singhtyaan@gmail.com (Tyaan Singh)
item.description: Check out the Github for this project This post is the first of a 3 part series. Part 1 - Scraping financial news headlines. Part 2 - Performing sentiment analysis to get sentiment scores for each headline. Part 3 - Aggregating our headline sentiment scores to create a sentiment value for each day the market is open, and checking for correlation between this and the stock price movement over the following day. This project is the first addition to my data science portfolio. The purpose of this project is to showcase web scraping, sentiment analysis, and some basic statistical analysis.

item.title: Sentiment Analysis of Financial News Headlines with Python - Part 1
item.author: singhtyaan@gmail.com (Tyaan Singh)
item.description: Check out the Github for this project This post is the first of a 3 part series. Part 1 - Scraping financial news headlines. Part 2 - Performing sentiment analysis to get sentiment scores for each headline. Part 3 - Aggregating our headline sentiment scores to create a sentiment value for each day the market is open, and checking for correlation between this and the stock price movement over the following day. This project is the first addition to my data science portfolio. The purpose of this project is to showcase web scraping, sentiment analysis, and some basic statistical analysis.
